_comment: Hand-curated EVENT-tier Pokemon. event_games = games where this Pokemon was ONLY obtainable via a real-world Mystery Gift or promotional event (no longer reproducible). non_event_games = games where it IS normally obtainable (overrides event status).

mew.note: Gen 1 Nintendo Power/CES promotional event (1996–1999). Available via My Pokemon Ranch (Platinum, JP), and Pokemon GO transfer in later games.

celebi.note: Distribution-only mythical. Crystal (US/EU) via Nintendo Space World event. Ilex Shrine in HeartGold/SoulSilver requires GS Ball event item (JP only).

jirachi.note: Colosseum Bonus Disc (US/EU), Channel (JP), various Mystery Gift events.

deoxys.note: Aurora Ticket via Mystery Gift in FRLG (event ended 2005). Normally obtainable in Emerald (Birth Island, also via Aurora Ticket but Emerald allows it via e-reader in JP) and ORAS (Sky Pillar area).

darkrai.note: Member Card Mystery Gift in DPPt (event ended 2008). Normally available in BDSP via Darkrai Oak's Letter.

shaymin.note: Oak's Letter Mystery Gift in DPPt (event ended 2009). Available normally in BDSP.

arceus.note: Azure Flute Mystery Gift (never officially distributed in DPPt). Hall of Origin. Normally obtainable in BDSP and Legends: Arceus.

victini.note: Liberty Pass Mystery Gift in BW — was a timed event but the Pokemon itself is normally obtainable in-game via the Pass.

keldeo.note: Distribution-only in Gen 5/6. Normally available in Sword/Shield via Isle of Armor DLC.

genesect.note: Distribution-only in Gen 5/6. Available via Pokemon HOME events in Gen 8.

volcanion.note: Serial code / distributions (2016). Never normally obtainable in-game.

magearna.note: QR code scan in SM/USUM (QR scanner functionality now disabled). Available via Pokemon HOME in Gen 8.

marshadow.note: Serial code distribution tied to Pokemon the Movie: I Choose You (2017).

zeraora.note: Serial code event distributions (2018, 2020). Never normally obtainable in-game.

meltan.note: Obtainable via Pokemon GO Mystery Box transfer to Let's Go. Not a typical event Pokemon — included for accuracy.

melmetal.note: Evolved from Meltan via 400 candies in GO, then transferred. Also via Pokemon HOME (Crown Tundra era).

zarude.note: Serial code tied to Secrets of the Jungle movie and Pokemon Jungle promotional codes (2020–2021).

glastrier.note: Crown Tundra DLC — normally obtainable but version-exclusive path.

spectrier.note: Crown Tundra DLC — normally obtainable but version-exclusive path.
